objectProperties.propertyDescription: The building in an address

objectProperties.propertyDescription: The name of the adjuvant

classes.description: An adjuvant to be included in the tank mix. In future this should be modelled as a subclass of Registered Formulation, by for now adjuvant digital labels are out of scope

objectProperties.propertyDescription: The description and guidance relating to an adverse effect (e.g. phytotoxicity)
The crop impacted by the specified adverse effects
The type of adverse effect, most typically phytotoxicity or varietal susceptibility

classes.description: The identified adverse effects of the registered formulation

classes.label: Agent

objectProperties.propertyDescription: The name of the person or organisation
A contact email address from the organisation
A contact fax number from the organisation, included for territories where fax remains a legally recognised communication medium and digital communications are unrecognised
The address of a person or organisation
The phone number/s associated with the agent
The url of the agent website
Flags organisations used by the platform to manage access control

classes.description: A person or organisation

classes.label: Application

objectProperties.propertyDescription: The type of application method.
Text placeholder for the full content on the label, to ensure no information is lost as it is encoded into structured data
The specified site/location and time for applying the product
The product application dosage expression with associated provenance
The minimum and/or maximum times the product should be used in a given time interval
The duration of time expressed as dates, growth stages or higher level lifecycle events between which the registered formulation should be applied.
The recommended duration between applications where applicable
The authorised range of absolute dose rates in mass of substance per unit of ground area
Adjuvant products which should be added to the tank mix
The adjuvant amount to be added to the application mix
The product application water amount per treatment
Flags whether it is safe to apply the product before or during frost
The minimum and/or maximum temperature within which the product should be used.
The default nozzle drift reduction classes to be used for spraying
Text placeholder for the spraying instructions, to ensure no information is lost as it is encoded into structured data
The equipment requirements for product application
The preparation instructions for the formulation as they apply to this direction for use
Information regarding other products which can and/or can't be safely used in the tank mix in combination with the formulation
Any products which should explicitly not be added into the tank mix due to their impact on the current product
Post-application equipment cleaning instructions.
The safety interval to be observed between application and harvesting of the crop
The safety interval to be observed between application and access to the treated area by animals (e.g. grazing livestock)
The safety interval to be observed between application and access to the treated area by humans
The safety interval to be observed between application to the ground and subsequent sowing or planting of the crop
The safety interval to be observed between application and successional sowing or planting of the next crop
Any additional safety intervals
The maximum authorised planting density for use with the product
The maximum authorised seeding rate for use with the product
The minimum or maximum seed sowing depth
Any usage guidance post-application e.g. for seed treatments

Efficacy information for the formulation on the specified target

The date for which the DFU is valid to, if different to the overall authorisation valid to date

classes.label: FormulationIngredient

objectProperties.propertyDescription: A substance that is used as an ingredient in the product formulation. As per EU Directive No 547/2011 Annex 1 section 1c ' The name must be as given in the list contained in Annex VI to Regulation (EC) No 1272/2008 of the European Parliament and of the Council (2) or, if not included therein, its ISO common name. If the latter is not available, the active substance shall be designated by its chemical designation according to IUPAC rules'
The role performed by the substance in the formulation (e.g. active substance, synergist, safener) - todo: review for controlled vocab
The concentration of an ingredient used in the product formulation
Flags whether the ingredient is of a high purity grade

objectProperties.propertyDescription: The quantity value, used when not a range

The lower inclusive limit of the quantity (equivalent to: greater than or equal to), that can be used with or without another upper limit range property but not with a <code>quantity.value<code>
The lower exclusive limit of the quantity, that can be used with or without another upper limit range property but not with a <code>quantity.value<code>
The upper exclusive limit of the quantity, that can be used with or without another lower limit range property but not with a <code>quantity.value<code>

classes.label: RegisteredFormulation

objectProperties.propertyDescription: The trade name of the product
The country or territory in which the product is authorised
The manufacturer of the registered formulation if different from the authorisation holder
The organisation who supply or market the product if different from the authorisation holder
The brand under which the product is marketed if different from the authorisation holder name
Regulatory authorisation information for the product
The high-level product type (insecticide, herbicide, fungicide or growth regulator) - todo: review for controlled vocab
The preparation type of the formulation
The material state/s of the product
The active substances, synergists and safeners which are included in the product
Any statement regarding coformulants if required in a territory
The product package information
Usage directions for the registered formulation
Distribution guidance relating to the transportation of the product
Guidance on how the product should be stored
The temperature range under which the product can be safely stored
The temperature range under which the product can be safely transported
Guidance concerning the safe disposal of the product
The product risk assessment and known hazards
The safety information including precautions and medical information related to the safe use of the registered formulation
The identified adverse effects of the registered formulation
Any introductory text, product summary or general overview information which appears on the label
A textual description of the mode of action of the complete formulation

Any additional crop rotation guidance. Information regarding phytotoxicity to successional crops should also be captured via adverse effects. Information relating to resistance management should also be captured under resistance management information.
Guidance relating specifically to successional sowing in the target field
Any guidance relating to crop failure.
An integrated pest management statement
The organisation to be contacted by growers for technical support, if specified
The colour of the formulation (provided as a check for the grower in case the product package has been reused)
The odour of the formulation (provided as a check for the grower in case the product package has been reused)
The UN number for the formulation
The storage classification code for the formulation
Any other mandatory information about the registered formulation which must be displayed on the label
Any additional advisory information on the label that does not belong on any other property in the model
Link to any additional instructions, which must be available for consumption or download online

classes.description: A crop protection product formulation that has been registered in a country or region

objectProperties.propertyDescription: The authorised uses of the product
The restrictions and explicitly prohibited uses of the product or its packaging

A local legal entity representing the authorisation holder if the authorisation holder is registered in another territory
The legal statutes or decrees under which the authorisation has been issued
The date from which the formulation authorisation is valid
The date up until which the formulation authorisation is valid

The contact responsible for product packaging, if different from the authorisation holder. As per EU Directive No 547/2011 Annex 1 section 1b, 'if different, the name and address of the person responsible for the final packaging and labelling or for the final labelling of the plant protection product on the market'

The contact responsible for product labelling, if different from the authorisation holder. As per EU Directive No 547/2011 Annex 1 section 1b, 'if different, the name and address of the person responsible for the final packaging and labelling or for the final labelling of the plant protection product on the market'
The person or organisation authorised to sell the registered formulation. As per EU Directive No 547/2011 Annex 1 section 1b, 'the name and address of the holder of the authorisation'
Any disclaimer stated on the label
